title: WebEx: Taking Conferencing to the Business Communications Summit
author: Aberdeen Group
source_file: 2002 WebEx- Taking Conferencing to the Summit.txt
abstract: An Aberdeen Group profile of WebEx Communications examining its web conferencing platform, private Mediatone network infrastructure, and new Training Center service. The study introduces a five-level model of real-time business communications, argues that WebEx is more akin to a telephone utility than a software company, and assesses how the post-9/11 environment and 2001 recession are accelerating the shift from face-to-face to web-based meetings. Published at a moment when WebEx had over 5,000 enterprise customers and was pulling decisively ahead of roughly 40 competitors.
importance_rationale: Early comprehensive profile of WebEx as the web conferencing category leader; documented the post-9/11 business communications shift that proved transformative; provides a clear benchmark of the market at a pivotal inflection point before Cisco acquisition and the rise of Zoom/Teams.
relevance_rationale: Web conferencing is now ubiquitous infrastructure; WebEx's specific Mediatone architecture is dated but the category it defined (now Zoom/Teams/Cisco Webex) is central to modern work. The competitive dynamics described are historically significant but the technology layer has been superseded.
prescience_rationale: The 'tectonic shift' prediction from face-to-face to web conferencing proved spectacularly correct, permanently accelerated by COVID-19 in 2020; WebEx's market leadership sustained through the $3.2B Cisco acquisition in 2007; virtually every claim about the direction of business communications proved accurate over the following two decades.
